clean_body: | | | | Welcome to "The Oregonizer" In 2020, we are titling our weekly news roundup, so watch for it on Wednesdays! 2020 is here! As you know, the stakes in this election year couldn't be higher. We have 104 days until April 21, the last day to register voters for the May 19 primary. Let's get out there and bring people into the process, into the party, and into the fight! The most effective way to do it is on the doors. Our next Eugene canvas Thanks for your support and Happy New Year everyone! Our first neighborhood canvas was a huge success! Volunteers knocked on doors in South Eugene, spreading the word about Canning for Congress, registering voters, and gathering signatures. All but two of our volunteers had never canvased before! Everyone had a great experience, and said they'd do it again. It's fun, its effective, and its a great way to get out and win this election. Join us for our next Canvas! Saturday January 18, meeting at HQ, 1492 West 6th in Eugene. | | IN THE NEWS | Doyle sits down with Springfield's Own Spent the Rent Podcast Special thanks to Patrick at for a wide ranging conversation covering everything from the climate crisis, homelessness and war with Iran -- to Doyle's love of Oregon, her two kids, and the power of stories. Tune in wherever you get your podcasts, and share! | #PursuePeace #NoWarWithIran Thanks to 150 people who came out with less than a day's notice to protest possible war with Iran! After reaching out to several peace organizations in Eugene, it was clear that no one was organizing an emergency rally on Saturday as part of the national mobilization to stop this war called by a broad coalition of dozens of groups like Code Pink. It was uplifting to see so many peaceloving people come out in the cold and stand together to condemn Trump's reckless, criminal, dangerous escalations in the Middle East. Our campaign will continue to speak out and support grassroots protest to end the forever wars, and stop any new wars! | Supporter Steve Kiernan of Common Defense, speaks out against war: "Its been 13 years since I got my legs blown off in Iraq...It seems the only thing we have to hand down to our children is more war. I don't know about you, but I don't want to live in a country like that. I don't want to live in a world like that." | Doyle spoke about the impact of the "forever wars" in Iraq and Afghanistan on her generation, and called for an immediate end escalations towards war in Iran. She continued: "And we must do more than just stop war with Iran. We must firmly commit to ending U.S. military presence in the Middle East in an orderly manner. We must end our involvement in the Saudi-led intervention in Yemen. We must bring our troops home from Afghanistan. This is what I have spent my entire adult life fighting for, and I'm not stopping now." | Rajeev Ravisankar, VP for external relations of the Graduate Teaching Fellows Federation (GTFF), says "As working class people...we have more in common with the people of Iran than with the billionaires profiting from war." #StopJordanCove | South coast residents united against the Jordan Cove fracked gas pipeline and LNG export terminal in Coos Bay gathered last night to voice opposition to the dredging permits requested by fossil fuel company Pembina. It was an overflow crowd with many supporters huddled outside listening to a livestream of the proceedings. In a tragically predictable outcome, the Coos Bay City Council voted to approve the permit 4 to 3, with Mayor Joe Benetti casting the tie breaking vote. (Benetti has long been a booster for Jordan Cove and has worked closely with Pembina to push the project forward, despite dogged opposition from the community.) | While local fisherman and others rallied in the cold against the blasting of the bedrock in the bay, and the permanent destruction of vital eel grass habitats that sustain local fisheries, and youth pled for a livable future.... | Jordan Cove cynically bussed in about 150 "supporters" from labor unions, LiUna, LIWU, and UFCW local 555, along with their team of lawyers. It was heartbreaking to see the divide and conquer astroturfing tactics on display. | Doyle spoke with KMTR about her strong opposition to Jordan Cove and her vision for a Green New Deal to bring good jobs to the South Coast in renewable wind and wave energy -- not the dead end of fossil fuels. We need that vision today more than ever. | ANNOUNCEMENTS | FELLOWSHIPS! JOIN THE TEAM Your movement, your campaign, join our fellowship program today! Our Fellowship program serves two purposes: to elect new leadership in Oregon 04, and to train new leaders in electoral politics. Fellows join a scrappy network of passionate grassroots leaders. The program is open to all ages! | UPCOMING EVENTS Join supporters to reach out to voters to tell them about our campaign. This event repeats weekly on Wednesdays. Join Students for Canning as they call voters to tell them about what's at stake in this election! SAVE THE DATE: DOYLE'S 40th BIRTHDAY PARTY FUNDRAISER Join Doyle and fellow Capricorn supporters Lauren Regan, Jamil Jona, and Melissa Carino, for a birthday party fundraiser. Stay tuned for updates on time, location, and ticket information! | YES WE CAN... | Q4 FUNDRAISING - THANK YOU! Special thanks to all of our grassroots donors who chipped in before December 31, making Q4 our most successful fundraising period yet. Stay tuned for our FEC report. | #DIVESTFROMWAR Doyle is a proud signatory of the Code Pink Pledge to accept no money from weapons makers and war profiteers! | DON'T FORGET TO REGISTER! 2020 is here, and Oregon has a closed primary system. subject: we're currently being outraised
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY PETERS FOR MICHIGAN Peters for Michigan
body:
Despite unprecedented support for our campaign in Michigan, my opponent just announced he has broken fundraising records and outraised us for the second straight reporting period.

---
| |
---
Friend, this is bad: Despite unprecedented support for our campaign in Michigan, my opponent just announced he has broken fundraising records and outraised us for the second straight reporting period. This is all thanks to Donald Trump funneling MILLIONS into Michigan to defeat me and buy this seat to expand Mitch McConnell's majority in the Senate.

**They know that Democrats have ZERO chance of taking back the Senate if we lose in Michigan. And now, a recent poll shows me statistically tied with my right-wing opponent.**

Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight reports that Democrats can take back the Senate, but we must first win in Michigan. That's why my team set a rapid-response goal of raising $20,000 by midnight - but right now, we're falling $5,100 short, and we're running out of time.

**Friend, in a race this close - and with just hours left until midnight - whether or not we hit this goal could determine Senate control, so I'm counting on your immediate contribution:**

**[Will you rush $3 right now- every dollar you can before midnight - to help us win in Michigan, take back the Senate for Democrats and protect the future of the Supreme Court?](https://secure.petersformichigan.com/page/m/7b6da941/5721a3ed/705f0f98/efb571f/1148794586/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoicGV0ZXJzX2VtYWlsIiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJqdGsyNDQtZnItaW4tbmQiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwLDUwMCIsImUzc2dZV0p5WldOMWNuSnBibWNnZlgwPSI6IiZyZWN1cnJpbmc9MSJ9/?g=SkKTSlNw52hcsC2e00JiNrA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/peters_email?refcode=jtk244-fr-in-nd&amounts=3,25,50,100,250,500&recurring=1")**
